nodes.description: Another fantastic example of a client handing over full creative control. I'm very happy with how this entire piece ended up from start to finish. Going into this project I knew the deliverables of the 16x9, 1x1, and 9x16 vertical was going to play a role in how I could setup my scenes. There are a few strong visual concepts that I was able to successfully incorporate into this spot that I am really excited about. The projection of the website and reflection of the water, and the playlist that I had incorporated from a previous spot ended up being cut for timing reasons. Anytime I have a chance to play with Octane renderer, I take full advantage of the power and try to see how realistic I can make my scenes. I am happy with the outcome. Below I am including a different spot that I created for this same client earlier. And yes, that is my voice as this was the version I was happiest with before we ended up rearranging and cutting pieces back to fit as the script was being reworked.
nodes.title: Another fantastic example of a client handing over full creative control.

nodes.description: The opportunity to have 100% creative control of the vision of a project is rare and at this magnitude it is unheard of. So I was really excited to let my creative freedom shine with this intro video for Nadeshot given the opportunity to have full control and trust. Inspired by modern, clean, design trends and the 100 Thieves swagger, I was motivated to tell the story of his rise to the top of esports through perseverance - it was a special story to tell through conceptual designs. I animated the CGI in Cinema 4D and the final project was compiled and finished in After Effects. Most importantly this project served as a testing ground for me using OTOY's new RNDR network to render almost instantaneously with 10,000 samples in extremely complex and unoptimized scenes.
nodes.title: The opportunity to have 100% creative control of the vision of a project is rare...

nodes.description: When creative freedom is given – you have to run with it. Here is an intro video for a popular League of Legends Streamer that turned into a great opportunity to change my workflow up a bit and test the waters out in Unreal Engine. What a fantastic tool for kitbashing and environment creation. The foliage tool and the rendering engine leveraging the sequencer is a massive game changer when brought into After Effects. I had to get craft with how I tracked the camera and used EXR information to squeeze out some mattes from the passes I rendered from Unreal. The models were created by my talented friend, Jeffrey Halalay, in ZBrush and brought into Element 3D for me to work into my scene. Also wanted to give a shout-out to www.productioncrate.com for having really helpful compositing effects and sound designs.
nodes.title: When creative freedom is given – you have to run with it.

nodes.description: Soak up the sun.
nodes.title: Soak up the sun.

nodes.description: Recently created a quick turnaround presentation video to showcase the ultimate FPS Stream Deck Icon pack we have created to give away for free at VBI. The visual design and the concept for this video was sparked from the idea I originally had to scope up and shoot the Stream Deck and have it do the same to us. I am very happy with how this turned out from an idea I had to this.
nodes.title: Recently created a quick turnaround presentation video to showcase the ultimate ...

nodes.description: Here is a quick turnaround product explainer video which includes character creation and animation. There was a great moment on the initial client consultation where we mentioned the idea and briefly explained it and it was a wonderful ah-ha moment for everyone on the call to kick off with confidence. I love when projects can begin and end with momentum!
nodes.title: Here is a quick turnaround product explainer video which includes character crea...

nodes.description: ASUS is doing some exciting things for professional gamers. Here is a promotional spot for their new ROG monitors and GeForce cards. Filmless was awesome on set and the Phantom slow motion camera was a great idea to showcase the comparison.
nodes.title: ASUS is doing some exciting things for professional gamers.

nodes.description: There's a journey with every client to learn how to tell their story in a way that they will appreciate and within their budget. This project was a great effort to achieve an animation within scope of the budget. I decided it would be best if we created a video with pose-driven illustrations and camera movement with depth and parallax.
nodes.title: There's a journey with every client to learn how to tell their story in a way th...

nodes.description: Extremely happy with how the latest spot turned out!  It's hilarious! Great to work with such awesome clients and an amazing producer at Foster Productions.
nodes.title: Extremely happy with how the latest spot turned out!

nodes.description: Every video project has its own interesting creative solutions. Especially when the client has specific ideas in mind. This promotional video has a fun scene while transitioning out of the graphical world into the office and out to the cityscape for the brand identity.
nodes.title: Every video project has its own interesting creative solutions.

nodes.description: As a co-owner and Head of Product I own the product vision and I'm responsible for the marketplace and everything on it. From concept to completion and into commercialization, I direct the overall operations and cross organization communication for the product vision. From site development and store features, to product planning, research & development, product innovation, team leadership, and marketing, I work to ensure a customer-focussed offering for the Stream Store. I oversee the KPI's and analytics in order to to identify the friction between our products and our customers as well as ensure a lasting quality experience. I work across the organization to launch innovative tools and beloved products on our marketplace. My many years of visual storytelling, cultivating partnerships, directing innovative products, and leadership experience has brought me the opportunity to help pave the way for gamers, creators, and brands as we redefine broadcast entertainment.
nodes.title: Co-owner & Head of Product at Visuals by Impulse / PZ

nodes.description: Full-service creative design team that delivers top-notch motion graphics with major players in the marketing field: Google, Disney, Nickelodeon, and Sony PlayStation. Personal roles include directing, producing, and animating.  Key projects include documentary and narrative films, marketing campaigns and animations.  Newest ventures include Virtual Reality content for North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences and 360 exhibit for North Carolina Museum of Art.  Most recently awarded EPIC mega grant for NatureXR, to continue expanding the limits of nature development and simulation within the Unreal Engine, including automated forests from Lidar scanning. We are a limitless creative company with solutions for all platforms including apps, game animation, live content, product tutorials and film.
nodes.title: CO-OWNER • DIRECTOR • ANIMATOR at Doctrine

nodes.description: As the Director of the Videezy stock marketplace, I cultivated a community of artists and users working to simplify their creative workflow. I built and managed a remote team of international artists and contributors where we created and distributed creative content. Directly under the CEO, I worked very closely to improve the product by launching site features, business strategies, and products in an agile environment. I developed and maintained valuable partnerships with other leaders in the stock industry, directed high quality studio productions, and implemented the processes for template creators and stock contributors. I wrote and implemented the video guidelines for contributors and footage reviewers and created SVG animations for the sites, and collaborated to build the Content Manager and the Automated Enrollment Funnel.
nodes.title: DIRECTOR OF VIDEO CONTENT at Eezy

nodes.description: Media Content Creator for apps, games, animations and films.  Worked with in-house clients on the post-production process of feature films including Fireproof and Letters to God.  Also created commercial work for Subway, SeaWorld, and Disney.  Final products included a variety of platforms including apps, games, animations, and films.
nodes.title: VIDEO EDITOR • ANIMATOR • MOTION DESIGNER at DNP Studios

nodes.description: Produced weekly television shows with live action content.  Created animated shorts, supervised and guided interns, managed budget, purchased equipment for studio, and designed and built sets for live television broadcasts.
nodes.title: Video Editor & Production Assistant at Access Osceola (AOTV)

nodes.description:
Sizzle Reels excite me and get my blood flowing. Here is a vast array of projects from my clients over the years showcasing my talents.
See other project pages for specific roles on projects that are pictured.

I co-own and direct a full-service creative design and video team that delivers top-notch motion graphics with major players in the marketing field: Google, Disney, Nickelodeon, and Sony PlayStation. I have been hands-on in directing and/or animating the projects seen in the Sizzle Reel. At Doctrine, I lead a limitless creative company that has solutions for all platforms including apps, game animation, live content, product tutorials and film.

tags: A video showcasing many years of work

nodes.title: 2019 Sizzle Demo Reel

nodes.description:
Working with top caliber brands that have strict brand guidelines is special because it provides a box to work in that allows creative freedom in an entirely different way than without the clear restrictions.
These animated pieces pushed the boundaries of that box and expanded the already existing guidelines. The HPE team loved these animations so much and appreciated that we raised the bar and added so much character to the animations.

Sometimes it is best to know the box you are working with. HPE has some of the best brand awareness that I have ever experienced. Having the experience to work with the HPE brand team has lead me to value and appreciate strict brand guidelines. It's easy to think that they are there to restrict your creative freedom, when really they can allow you to be just as creative and in fact much quicker because you already know what you are working with and the answers to a lot of scenarios are right in the brand guide.
nodes.title: Brand Awareness - HPE

nodes.description:
This fully animated TV commercial was brought from it's original conceptualization to completion in 2 weeks with a rigorous delivery schedule.
My process was clear and simple and the team at TWA were quick to deliver feedback. From design mockups to a pose based storyboard, this project proved the importance of a structured workflow.

Having the opportunity to work with the team at Toho Water Authority again I knew we were going to have some fun. The vision for this project was pretty clear and coming together with the style and design of the character it was important to keep in mind the animated actions and expressions that the script was indicating.

On an animated project like this, one of the best ways to get through to any team and/or client is to determine timing and deliver a storyboard edit. This is usually when things all come together. The storyboard edit lays the groundwork for all work ahead such as timing out the characters actions, to music cues and sound design.

When approached to create a cartoon for a really fun idea, our creative gears started spinning out of control. We quickly set forth to write a script with the client to their satisfaction. We ended up with something silly and it set the stage for the design and animation style. Imagining a day without water infrastructure is something that a lot of people don't think about so the client decided to go with a day in the life of one of their own employees which was a neat idea. Everyone in their offices loves it and it played well on their social media pages.

tags: We Cannot Make It Rain • Imagine A Day Without Water

nodes.title: Character Animated Commercial

nodes.description:
Over the years I've found that I learn a lot about myself and my visual style by creating multiple demo reels. By making the demo reel a story in and of itself is where the fun begins.
My flow state occurs when I am editing this kind of video because I love to create. My passion is providing an experience whether it be through storytelling or interactivity.  It's what drives me to pursue all avenues.
nodes.title: Demo Reel - Visual Storytelling

nodes.description:
In 2015 when Bodymovin was introduced into After Effects I quickly jumped right in with ideas on how to improve the product at Eezy along with the CEO, Shawn Rubel. I created animations for the 404 pages across all sites and recreated the Vecteezy Editor with shape layers in order to animate a quick walkthrough in SVG.
A fun fact that very few people know, that I learned from Hernan Torissi, who is the creator of Bodymovin and collaborator of Lottie, originally set out to create the plugin/feature because of the extinction of Flash. And it's called Bodymovin because this animation data lives within the 'body' of the webpages HTML and also because it is a Beastie Boys song.

In 2015 when Bodymovin was introduced into After Effects I quickly jumped right in with ideas on how to improve the product at Eezy along with the CEO, Shawn Rubel. I created animations for the 404 pages across all sites and recreated the Vecteezy Editor with shape layers in order to animate a quick walkthrough in SVG.

It took the creator of Bodymovin, Hernan Torissi, years to complete his plugin for After Effects and he shared it with the world for free. He's a pioneer that created it out of sheer necessity after Flash accepted its death note.

A fun fact that very few people know, that I learned from Hernan himself, who is also the collaborator of Lottie at Airbnb, is that he dubbed it Bodymovin because the animation or "movin" data lives within the 'body' of the webpage HTML and also because it is a Beastie Boys song.

www.vecteezy.com/editor

tags: Vector animations are native across all devices

nodes.title: Code Based Animation - SVG

nodes.description:
Todd Graves is Moe Norman's understudy and he teaches the practices of the Single Plane Swing.
This interactive application will be used for Todd's presentations he gives around the globe to better compare the bio-mechanical differences between a conventional golf swing and his single plane swing.
I had an opportunity to work with Studio 500, previously Vicon/House of Moves, one of the nations best/largest Motion Capture studios in Downtown Orlando.
The comps seen here were created by Derek Alan Rowe and the Plotting seen here by Jeffrey Halalay.  I work with a wonderful team.
nodes.title: Motion Capture - Golf Swing App

nodes.description:
For two years in a row Star Wars fans gathered to Orlando's convention center for the largest Star Wars convention there is, Star Wars Celebration.
Being commissioned the previous year as well we knew going into CVI from CV we needed to push the limits and really give these fans and members of the 501st Legion something special.  After all this was their 15th anniversary.
After filming the entire celebration and editing together roughly 20 videos for the limited edition DVD we then set out to design and develop an iOS App of the interactive experience.
nodes.title: Interactive iOS App and DVD

nodes.description:
Neil Patrick Harris needed an animated logo for the ending of his latest TV show "Best Time Ever" on NBC.  So who better for the task than myself :-D
No but seriously it was pretty awesome working with NPH and Zoë on this project :P
nodes.title: Network TV - Logo Animation

nodes.description: I was given full creative control on this show open.  My goal was to appeal to other Call Of Duty:  Black Ops players but also keep it short enough to not be annoying and also to brand it will with the Epic Dropshott logo which I also designed.
nodes.title: Show Intro - YouTube Gaming

nodes.description:
I was commissioned by Amazon Best Selling Author Brandt Legg to illustrate the book covers to his new series The Cosega Sequence.
Brandt had a really fantastic vision for these covers and in the end we both are extremely happy with the result.
"You are brilliant!  I'm amazed. How do you bring into reality the images in my mind?"  -Brandt Legg
nodes.title: Book Series Cover Design

nodes.description:
The sketches were provided by the client which I followed very closely.  The mission was to create a realistic illustration of a forge and of a logo.
Process:  I used a combination of software to achieve the final look.  Cinema 4D to model the environment, hammer, hearth, anvil, and stones.  Mixamo Fuse to create the blacksmith and finalize in Cinema 4D to match the lighting in order to composite.  Photoshop for final composite and finishing.  The coals, window, and lightning are all altered photography in order to get what I was looking for.  Optical Flares were used on the lightning as well.
nodes.title: CG Logo and Poster Design

nodes.description:
One of my favorite artists happens to be a good friend of mine, Raymond Klecker.
Here's a collaboration I'm doing him to color his daily sketches.  After all they are freakin' awesome.
nodes.title: Art Collaboration

nodes.description:
This past year there was a lot of buzz for a famous tap game "Flappy Bird" and the demand for a new one once the creator pulled it off of the App Store.
In the midst of all of this I wanted to create my own take on the famous tap game by creating the very best physics based flappy there is.
At the same time partnering with Skillz to allow the players to compete for Z's (fake currency) or cold hard cash (actual juicy $).
Along with this experience of getting my very own game on the App Store I also had a great experience creating a pitch letter, press release, and landing page along with a facebook page.
The above mentioned can all be accessed by the URL below.
nodes.title: iOS Game - Good Luck Duck

nodes.description:
For over 6 years my company has been working with Disney to create all kinds of video projects from commercials, internet marketing, digital signage, and much more.
Here are a few examples of the type of work we do with Disney.
nodes.title: Video Productions - Disney

nodes.description: An astronaut who is lost in space is visited by a being from another dimension. The being shares his knowledge of the universe which forever changes the perspective of the astronaut. The astronaut no longer sees himself as "lost."
nodes.title: Animated Visual Storytelling

nodes.description:
Walt Disney World Resort is home to the newest and largest Four Seasons Hotel.  The lobby has it's very own Disney Information Counter where Disney Cast Members will answer questions and get you started on your Disney experience while you are visiting.
The task was to create a minimal/themed display for digital signage for this new Disney Counter.
This displays the process of this creation from start to finish.
nodes.title: Digital Signage - Four Seasons

nodes.description:
LYT was born from the idea of a relaxing endless tap game from the Flappy Bird era. It was a hack-a-thon weekend that Derek, Jeff, Gatlin, and I had at the office and it was never fully completed.
It was a super satisfying game to play as you press and hold the distance between your spiraling lights increases. You can hear us discussing the way your lights life will be diminished as you press and hold.
Before "Storm the Train" was released for iOS I realized there was a lack of quality side-scroller shooters in the App Store.
This is where "Intra" was born.  It's still a work-in-progress but some day, hopefully very soon, I will be releasing this bad boy to the world of mobile gaming.

tags: LYT Endless Tap • Intra Side Scroller

nodes.title: Unity Game Design - iOS

nodes.description:
An animation currently being displayed behind all Johnson Controls booths at car industry conventions.
It's to inform manufacturers of the device we all have in our cars called "HomeLink" and that it is now even more versatile and can be implemented into all models and makes of automobiles and can do much more than before.
nodes.title: Animation - Informational Display

nodes.description: Illustrations and artwork completed in 2014 for many different clients for very different reasons.
nodes.title: Illustrations and Artwork

nodes.description:
Since I was a young child I've been a storyteller and after attending Film School I went on to become a filmmaker and here are some of the feature films I have been a part of creating along with my brother.
There is so much that goes into making a film, especially a feature.  In 2008 Out On Parole was our very first feature length film and it was one of the greatest yet toughest things I've ever been a part of.  I worked with some incredibly talented people and we pulled off some miraculous scenes that took every ounce of our energy to get it right.
Out On Parole is currently being adapted into a web series for release in 2015.  We are working with the talented and amazing hip-hop group "The Solillaquists of Sound" to produce a custom soundtrack and score for the series/film.
Within Reach came shortly after Out On Parole and was our very first feature length documentary film.  We raised over $25,000 on Kickstarter for finishing funds and are currently working with a distribution partner to allow the world to know and see this fantastic film.  A film about a young couples bike journey across the country to visit over 100 sustainable communities hopefully to call one their home.  The distribution is in the screening kit stage and looking to move into internet rentals next month.  This film is currently being translated into 8 different languages and is also receiving a new website backend & makeover.
One Simple Question just had its World Premiere at the Blue Ocean Film Festival in Tampa, FL.  It's been submitted and accepted to Film Festivals across the country and I am excited to see it on the big screen again.
nodes.title: Feature Films

nodes.description: The Institute is responsible for cutting edge Medical Research and was looking for ways to raise funding and find partners.  This video was premiered at a Formal Gala at the Institutes Facility to do just that.
nodes.title: Compositing and 3D Animation

nodes.description:
While I was in Yosemite back in 2011 I had a vision for a mixture of Tetris and Scrabble because that sounds amazing.
After a few mockups I decided that the words and dictionary was over my head at the time so instead we created an alternate version using colors.
nodes.title: Game Development - Concept

nodes.description:
City of Dreams in Macau is home to Dragon's Treasure which is a totally immersive 360° multimedia journey.
Transporting you to another world as the four Dragon Kings explore the mysterious powers of the Dragon Pearl and introduce you to their magical kingdoms. It all takes place at The Bubble – a dome-shaped theater specially built for this show.
This is the Pre-Show that the audience watches before they enter into the main 360° theater.
nodes.title: CGI and 3D Animation

nodes.description: Designing motion is one of my strongest skill-sets and over the years I have had many opportunities to work on many projects ranging from commercials, music videos, and even projection art.
nodes.title: Demo Reel - Motion 2016

nodes.description:
My company Doctrine Creative partnered with The Kimball Companies for years to create a lot of material for the web and television.  With the focus to bring families back to the dinner table, I enjoyed working for such a great cause and amongst great people to create something special.
Here are also a few Family Philosopher clips we teamed with Carnegie Mellon Actors to bring a comedic vibe to the experience.
nodes.title: Video Marketing Campaign Series

nodes.description:
"The Doctrine Film Experience – My favorite panel of the day. It was all about becoming a filmmaker and encouraging people to just get out there and do it. They show how easy it is to make a film by adding to a Star Wars fan film with people from the audience. After filming for five minutes, the panelists took questions on how to get started, and finally ended with the scene they just filmed seamlessly added to their movie. The moviemakers and crew were really exciting, had lots of passion and by the end, everyone in the room felt like making a movie."
-Posted By Kevin Hopp on Thu, Mar 18, 2010 at 12:37 PM

tags: The End of Jar Jar Binks

nodes.title: Interactive Live Demo - MegaCon

nodes.description:
In 2010 my wife and I founded a SCRAPS Recycling to create awareness for recycling and to also revive local recycling programs.
Pepsi Refresh Everything Project was a great way for us to do just this.  We created a video submission and submitted to the project in hopes to get the grant.
nodes.title: Animated Fundraising Video

nodes.description:
Beelzebilly started a long time ago from a writer Mark Kelliher who contacted us who had previous success on cartoon network with a puppet show.  Mark became a good friend over the years and BZB was a slow process.  The audio has been completed for some time with some of the leading voice talent in the business.
BZB is currently in the process of becoming a short film cut together and animated in 3D instead of the original 2D concept.
In memory of Mark Kelliher.
nodes.title: Cartoon Pitch - Audio Only

nodes.description:
This is the opening for Youth Football in Steelers Country presented by McDonald's.
I was given a few stock videos of players and cheerleaders/referee and told to create an opening for a television show for youth football.
Here is what I made.
nodes.title: Animated TV Show Intro

nodes.description:
The Full Frame Documentary Film Festival is the nations largest festival for documentaries.
In 2010 my company created this animation which played before every single film that was premiered at the festival as a way to set the mood for the theater.
It was also was played before 'Food Inc.' on the World Premiere night and that was really special.

nodes.description: An international company created a silly mobile app for customizing and accessorizing celebrities that dangerously resemble humpty dumpty.  Then they found me and wanted to create a fun/hip music video cartoon to help market it throughout the internet.  Wahlah.
nodes.title: Cartoon Animation

nodes.description:
Winner of a 2011 Telly Award for "Best use of graphics." My company Doctrine Creative and I created all CG elements and animated this piece together fluently.
DNP Studios shot the green-screen footage which we composited into the video and Say It Loud provided the original Storyboards.
nodes.title: 3D Animated Corporate Video

nodes.description: Suits
nodes.title: Suits

nodes.description:
One of the most meaningful moments of my life was when I told you my dreams of becoming a filmmaker, and you smiled—that soft, soulful smile—and said that your dream was to be a mother and to travel the world.

I didn’t fully understand why it moved me so deeply then. But I do now.
It’s because, in that moment, you were showing me a dream that was pure. And deep down, all I wanted was to be with that woman. 

A woman whose dream was to love her children with everything she had and to explore the world together in wonderment. 

And now… just let that sink in.

You’ve become that woman—and so much more.

Just look at you. You’ve not only become the woman you dreamed of—you’ve surpassed her in every way.
You’ve created a life that most people spend a lifetime hoping for.
But you didn’t stumble into it. You built it.

You’re now only 38, and already, you’ve painted a life more vivid and honest than most dare to dream.
Not through luck or chance—but by choosing it, nurturing it, becoming it.
You didn’t just chase your dream—you embodied it.
And in doing so, you’ve become a quiet revolution.
A woman who lives so fully in her truth, she awakens others simply by being herself.

This past year alone—you’ve led us from Greece to Maine, through Acadia’s wild beauty, and across Europe once again—Spain, Germany, Austria, Italy. A dream life that’s somehow real. Because you made it so.

And through it all, you’ve remained the strongest, most nurturing soul I’ve ever known.
You mother without asking for credit. You give without asking for thanks.
You plan, organize, carry, create—and you do it all with grace.
You are breathtaking in every way. And yet somehow, you continue to gain beauty with age.

I’m ready to admit that I had to grow to see what you saw all along.
And I’m so grateful I did—because this life we’ve built, this family, you—
It’s everything I never knew I wanted, until you showed me it was possible.

It is the greatest honor of my life to be your husband.

Happy Birthday to the woman who makes life the most beautiful adventure.
I love you beyond what words can hold.
nodes.title: One of the most meaningful moments of my life was when I tol...

nodes.description:
From the very beginning, you’ve made me smile easier than anyone.
You were such a funny baby…bright, quick, curious, and somehow, it felt like you were already here, fully formed, just figuring out how to walk and talk as fast as you could.

And you did. You’ve always moved fast. Thought fast. Eaten fast. And loved with your whole heart. 

You’ve spent more time on my shoulders than anywhere else, because that’s just where you fit right from the very start. And even now, when I close my eyes, I can still feel the weight of you there, your little chubby hands holding on, both of us just being together.

You have a heart full of joy, kindness, and this deep, emotional strength that not many people your age carry. You care so deeply about the people you love, and you protect them with everything you’ve got.

You’re creative, thoughtful, hilarious, and full of light. Whether you’re baking your gluten free double tree oat cookies or drawing with that wildly vivid imagination of yours, you put your whole heart into it. And that is so special, and it shows in everything you do.

Watching you with your brothers, or with your best friend Leo, is one of my favorite things in the world. That lemonade and orange juice stand you made outside your school, that wasn’t just cool that was magic.

And every time you ask me for a bedtime story, my heart just swells. You don’t know what that means to me. You don’t know what you mean to me. But someday you will.

My eyes fill when I think about all the moments that shaped you into the boy you are, and the man you’ll one day become. You are a gift. So much light, and so much joy!

It’s an honor to be your dad. Truly. What a pleasure to say I’m your Father, Son.

Happy golden birthday, Jole. I love you so, so much.
nodes.title: From the very beginning, you’ve made me smile easier than an...

nodes.description:
You’ve changed my life in more ways than I can ever explain. From the moment you were born, there was something different about you. Your eyes, your spirit, the way you looked at the world as if you already knew something most people don’t.

You’re the kind of person who makes the world better just by being in it. You lift people up without even trying. You bring calm. You bring laughter. You bring heart. Every single day, someone tells me how incredible you are, and every single time, I smile because I already know.

You’ve faced challenges that could have made you bitter or discouraged, but instead, you became stronger. You became wiser. You became more compassionate. You’ve shown me how to choose joy, even when it’s gluten free. You’ve shown your brothers what kindness looks like. You’ve shown the world what it means to live with a glass half full.

You’ve helped me become the father I always hoped to be. Watching you grow up has been the greatest honor of my life. And now, as you step into your teenage years, you’re not someone still trying to figure out who they are. You already know. And you’re not afraid to be that person. You’ll inspire others to do the same.

Wherever your journey takes you, just know I’m always walking right behind you. Cheering you on. Learning from you. Loving you more than words can hold.

I am so proud of you. And this world is better with you in it.

To the ends of the earth, happy birthday, Jace.
nodes.title: You’ve changed my life in more ways than I can ever explain....

nodes.title: An immortal gaze

nodes.description: These buildings are growing on me.
nodes.title: These buildings are growing on me.

nodes.title: This mural watches the sun rise.

nodes.title: This mural sings.

nodes.description:
Happy Birthday, Maria!

Yesterday was a perfect reflection of the life you lead—selflessly creating the most incredible birthday party for Jole, without a single mention of your own special day. Your beauty and thoughtfulness shine through in everything you do. Your natural ability to mother has become a superpower, one that you’ve earned through hard work and dedication. I’ve seen the effort you put in, silently and without complaint. You never hesitate, frown, or vent. The only way anyone would even know of the endless things you do for your family is by witnessing it firsthand—the boxes, the planning, the calendars, the emails, the books, the groceries, the toys, the candy, the art supplies. You are the most stunning and beautiful woman, and somehow, you keep winning mother of the year. As a father, it’s the greatest gift I could hope for our boys.

Being here in Greece with you is a testament to the relationship we’ve built. The moments we’ve shared in this life, and those we continue to create, mean the world to me. You’ve brought such joy, clarity, and meaning to my life, improving my mental and physical well-being every single day. Today, as we celebrate you, I want you to know how profoundly you impact not only me, but those around you. With you, the skies keep getting bluer, and the waters keeps getting clearer.
nodes.title:
Happy Birthday, Maria!

Yesterday was a perfect reflection o...

nodes.description:
Brother—it’s the most meaningful word I know. Having two brothers myself, I understand what it means to carry a part of someone else deep within your consciousness for your entire life. The feeling that without them, you’re not whole. With my three boys, I see this same connection.

I’m so fortunate to watch them take on new challenges and overcome old fears. They’re growing into confident, independent young men, stepping into new roles, and finding their own paths.

Despite their fights and unparalleled ability to get under each other’s skin, their bond is deep and unbreakable. They bring out the best in each other and have formed a strong support system, backed by blood, sweat, tears, and tickle fights.

C.S. Lewis said, ‘In each of my friends there is something that only some other friend can fully bring out.’ This couldn’t be more true, especially within brotherhood. My sons are not just growing up together, they’re growing into each other’s strengths and life stories. They are brothers in the most profound sense, and that’s something truly deserving of the most meaningful word I know.
nodes.title: Brother—it’s the most meaningful word I know. Having two bro...

nodes.description:
Just before beginning what would have been a year-long home renovation, that we had planned for years—we decided to leave the world we knew behind to start a new chapter, far from home. Along the way we expanded our understanding of what home really means. Home isn’t within new pavements or fancy tiles. Home is within us, in our togetherness. It grows richer with every new experience we share and every bit of beauty we take in. The more we learn to appreciate all of the beauty, the more we see of it…the more we experience it. 

The other night, Jace asked me, ‘Daddy, has living here changed you?’ I felt the entirety of that question, and after a thoughtful pause, I said, ‘Yes, it has.’ And before I could even finish, he burst out, ‘Me too!’ 

That moment will stay with me forever, melting my heart and sending the good shivers down my spine. What a wonderful world we get to live in.
nodes.title: Just before beginning what would have been a year-long home ...

nodes.description:
I tell myself I’m a storyteller, yet sometimes that feels like the only story I’m telling. I talk of achieving incredible things, yet I scarcely believe it myself. Belief—it’s a peculiar thing. It seems you only truly accomplish something once you deeply, unquestionably know that you will—that’s when you actually do it. It’s so common for us to identify with the stories we tell ourselves, the ones we choose to believe in. And eventually, we become the very characters we’ve described.

The narrative of our lives is being written, and inevitably, we’ll walk upon a ledge. We can stand there, peering over the edge, and if we do, the ending is as certain as the ground below. But there’s another choice. If we can convince ourselves to believe—to trust—that when we leap from this ledge, when we dive into the unknown, the world has actually been patiently waiting all this time to catch us on the other side and share with us all its beauty.

That’s a story I can tell, a true story. Because I’m a storyteller.
nodes.title: I tell myself I’m a storyteller, yet sometimes that feels li...

nodes.description: Off the coast of Syros, I find myself aboard a beautiful yacht. Surrounded by new friends who inspire me, who seek beauty and lean into laughter. It’s almost a mystery how I’ve arrived here. How a series of decisions led me to this exact moment. Yet, here I am, on a sailboat in Greece, amidst mountains of wisdom, a sea of love and support, and a crew of intentional souls. Everything is illuminated.
nodes.title: Off the coast of Syros, I find myself aboard a beautiful yac...

nodes.description:
To my wife, the mother of our children, beautiful, funny, and strong—You are the rock for our boys as you’ve always been for me.

Your love for reading, crafting, and traveling has set a wonderful example for them. Now, we find ourselves in Greece, absorbing a sea of culture, exploring pottery, and discovering new perspectives on the world.

Through your teachings and your passions, you have inspired our boys to be themselves and to bless the world with their incredible spirit and good intentions.

You say you are not creative, yet you have expertly guided these children with careful attention and clear intention. They have grown to be wholesome and real, well-behaved, and sometimes brave.

As an incredible mother, you’ve inspired them to rise to wherever the world might take them, to willingly confront challenges, and to always be there for each other.

Happy Mother’s Day.
nodes.title: To my wife, the mother of our children, beautiful, funny, an...

nodes.description:
Jace wrote a lyrical poem while we were in Naxos and I was moved by his words and wanted to share them with you. Turns out I was taking these photos while he was writing his song.

Live a Little
by Jace boy sits on a perfectly placed rock
in a perfectly placed area,
in a perfectly placed world,
in a perfectly placed universe,
waiting for the days to go by.

Years and years pass by,
and the boy had a thought from listening to what the other people would say,
“I realize that the only way to get through life is to live a little,
instead of waiting for the days to go by.”

So he gets up from his rock,
thinking of things to do while he’s away,
And once he figures it out,
he goes ahead and runs away.

As the man gets up from his rock,
another boy walks by and sits on the perfectly placed rock,
in the perfectly placed area,
in the perfectly placed world,
in the perfectly placed universe,
just waiting for the days to go by.
nodes.title: Jace wrote a lyrical poem while we were in Naxos and I was m...

nodes.description: Every challenge presents a call to leap, expanding our horizons. As we find the courage to jump, we inspire those around us and we eventually realize something beautiful. Our children aren’t just following—they’re bravely leading, not only us, but future generations into tomorrow.
nodes.title: Every challenge presents a call to leap, expanding our horiz...

nodes.description: Watching our boys dance with the waves and lay in the sand, I’m reminded of a different kind of investment. Not one where money multiplies into more money, but where it rolls up into cocoons and transforms—emerging as butterflies that bless the world. These blessings return as experiences that shape our character, change our perspectives, and guide us to the true riches of life.
nodes.title: Watching our boys dance with the waves and lay in the sand, ...

nodes.description: Tracing the paths of our childhood, my boys nearly mirror the adventures my brothers and I once had. Except, this time we are reunited in beautiful Naxos for Easter weekend, swapping the daily grind for quality time and a heated pool.
nodes.title: Tracing the paths of our childhood, my boys nearly mirror th...

nodes.description: Exploring ancient caves today and I found joy in the simple act of discovery. It’s moments like these where we are surrounded by history and beauty that spark the greatest conversations. How “fun” might just be a human perception of experimentation, and how each particle participates in this conscious behavior.
nodes.title: Exploring ancient caves today and I found joy in the simple ...

nodes.description:
With water as clear as the thoughts these times inspire, I’m reminded of how close ‘living’ and ‘loving’ are—not just in letters, but in every moment we spend together. 

I’m very grateful to have my mom and brothers visiting us here in Greece, sharing this beautiful journey. Here’s to living fully and loving deeply, surrounded by the beauty of the world and the warmth of my family.
nodes.title: With water as clear as the thoughts these times inspire, I’m...

nodes.description: To my oldest, Jace, I see you. Your passion for reading, your excitement to learn, and the love and honesty you share with us all—it doesn’t go unnoticed. You’re not just a son and a brother, you’re a role model, an inspiration. Every day, you show us what it means to be a good person, and for that, I couldn’t be more proud. I’m honored to be your guide. Keep being you, son. I love you.

I've always loved Indian cuisine but only recently have I started making Indian dishes. 

Coconut and kale quinoa with coconut kidney beans along with some pickled onions, korma sauce, and mustard seeds.

I love the simplicity of this dish and it's delicious. I usually slice up an avocado with this meal but they weren't ripe enough today.
